zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Silverside於1545年6月15日出生在英國賀特福爾賀特福德時，她的父親Thomas Silverside II25歲，母親Janet Grey18歲。她於1567年6月15日與Thomas Richardson III在英國賀特福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1634年7月31日在她的家鄉享年89歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾賀特福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Silverside于1545年6月15日出生在英国賀特福爾賀特福德时，她的父亲Thomas Silverside II25岁，母亲Janet Grey18岁。她于1567年6月15日与Thomas Richardson III在英国賀特福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1634年7月31日在她的家乡享年89岁，埋葬在英国賀特福爾賀特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Silverside
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: nickname from Middle English silver ‘silver’ + side ‘side (of the body, ?head)’. Compare Richard Silvereghe (‘silver eye’), 1414–15 in Inquisitiones post Mortem (Yorks); Adam Siluermouth (‘silver mouth’), 1379 in Yorks Poll Tax ; John Silvertop (‘silver hair’), 1478 in York Freemen’s Register . Some early bearers may belong with sense 2.
2  English: locative name from Silver Side in Farlam (Cumb), so recorded in 1485.

variants.name: Yorks Poll Tax
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name May + the patronymic marker -son.
2 English: a northern spelling of Mason . Compare Adam de Morton, mayson, 1371 in Yorks Poll Tax .

variants.name: York Freemen’s Register
meanings: 1  English: locative name, variant of   Rathmell  .   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames   notes that Thomas Routhmell was made a freeman of the city of York in 1521 (   York Freemen’s Register  ) and in the subsidy roll three years later he was named as Thomas Rodemelle. Redmonds also cites Ralph Radmille or Rodemell, 1598–1605 in   Parish Registers   (Brandesburton, ER Yorks).

2 English: locative name from Rodmell (Sussex), which appears to have survived chiefly as Ramell. The place, recorded as Ramelle, Redmelle in 1086 ( Domesday Book ) and Radmelde in 1202, is probably named with Old English rēad ‘red’ + mylde ‘soil’.

variants.name: Inquisitiones post Mortem
meanings: English: (i) locative name from Shilvington (Northumberland), recorded as Schillington, 1323 in Inquisitiones post Mortem . (ii) locative name perhaps also euphemistically for one or other place called Shitlington (WR Yorks and Beds, the latter now Shillington). Compare Rafe Shittleton, 1604 in IGI (Cheshunt, Herts), for the latter; and Williame Shitlingtoune, 1668 in IGI (Dumfries, Dumfriess), for the former.

variants.name: Silversides
meanings: English: variant of Silverside with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Silverside was born on 15 June 1545, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Thomas Silverside II, was 25 and her mother, Janet Grey, was 18. She married Thomas Richardson III on 15 June 1567, in Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 31 July 1634, in her hometown, at the age of 89, and was buried in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Silverside est née le 15 juin 1545, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Silverside II, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Janet Grey, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Richardson III le 15 juin 1567, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 31 juillet 1634, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 89 ans, et a été enterrée à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Silverside am 15. Juni 1545 in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Silverside II 25 und ihre Mutter Janet Grey 18. Sie hat am 15. Juni 1567 in Hertfordshire, England Thomas Richardson III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 31. Juli 1634 im Alter von 89 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Silverside è nata il il 15 giugno 1545 nel Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Silverside II, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Janet Grey, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Richardson III il 15 giugno 1567, nel Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 31 luglio 1634, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 89 anni, e fu sepolta a Hertford, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Silversideが1545年6月15日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードで生まれた時，父Thomas Silverside IIは25歳，母Janet Greyは18歳であった。1567年6月15日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアでThomas Richardson IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1634年7月31日に故郷で89歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Silverside(이)가 1545년 6월 15일 영국 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Silverside II(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Janet Grey(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1567년 6월 15일 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 Thomas Richardson III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1634년 7월 31일 그녀의 고향에서 89세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.15 June 1545–31 July 1634: 15 de junho de 1545–31 de julho de 1634
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Silverside nasceu em 15 de junho de 1545, em Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Silverside II, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Janet Grey, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Richardson III em 15 de junho de 1567, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 31 de julho de 1634, em sua cidade natal, com 89 anos, e foi sepultada em Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Silverside родилась 15 июня 1545 г. в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Silverside II было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Janet Grey, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Richardson III 15 июня 1567 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 31 июля 1634 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 89 лет и была похоронена в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.15 June 1545–31 July 1634: 15 de junio de 1545–31 de julio de 1634
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Silverside nació el 15 de junio de 1545, en Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Silverside II, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Janet Grey, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Thomas Richardson III el 15 de junio de 1567, en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 31 de julio de 1634, en su pueblo, a la edad de 89 años, y fue sepultada en Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
